review: Growing on me a lot, it does sound like other songs but the production is excellent for me, I love how it develops and she still sounds pretty good. The Transformers sounds in the beat does a lot for me also. It's fun, upbeat and catchy and more importantly a bit refreshing from her even if it's does remind me of others
label: positive

review: Now THIS is art. Definitely one of the best rock songs of the decade and maybe one of my personal favourite rock songs of all time. This is what I call PERFECTION. Her vocals are on point, the production is incredibly flawless and the composition is very interesting. I also love the lyrics.
label: positive

review: Superscar is an incredible opener and the cookie cutter, perfect song, while follow-ups like new single, while Machine Girl leans more into experimental territory. Homewrecked is an europop-inspired illegally catchy song whose chorus sticks to your brain,, displaying her underrated lyricism. My absolute favorite, though, is definetely still DeathByDevotion. The music video is still incredible, and the “nmixx, change up” on the last 20 seconds goes insanely hard. This definetely feels the most “ADÉLA” out of all the songs.
label: positive

review: So Close to What is barely an improvement over that. The improvement being that it’s awfully mid and slightly less annoying—nothing more. No artistic growth whatsoever. I wouldn’t have even written this review if it weren’t for a friend nagging at my ass, saying I was ignoring Tate. Thing is, I wasn’t ignoring her. And I knew exactly what to expect here. My "history", if you can even call it that, with Tate goes back to 2022 when I heard some tracks off her debut and thought they were okay. Nothing bad, but also just okay. It didn’t feel at the time like she was one of those indie singer-songwriter wannabes with zero talent, there was at least a glimpse of potential. A glimpse that completely disappeared the moment I heard Greedy and Exes from Think Later back in 2023. It became clear that Tate, then and now, had ditched that direction in favor of this lifeless, empty, and sexually driven type of contemporary R&B. Oh boy, what a turn. Not to suggest that her music was great in the first place, but as I said there was at least some potential there.
label: negative

review: So like the first and last two songs were kinda alright but the rest of the songs were either terribly boring or just quite bad otherwise. The subject matters in the lyrics got repetitive and annoying real fast, not that i’ve liked her lyrics at all in the past either. I swear every song has the same fucking topics you know could she write about anything else than men or sexual stuff. What a range in writing.

review: I found this to be quite underwhelming compared to Olivia's past work, especially what we got on Guts and Guts Spilled. Not awful, not amazing. Pretty middle of the road for me.
label: neutral

review: It's so painfully repetitive. Almost every song sounds the same, or at least has the same creative mindset behind it. I mean, we're getting some true target shopping center bangers, and if you get that reference, you would know that's not a compliment. And there's really nothing daring about this record besides maybe some very subtle hip hop influence in the mix, at least the ones I would pick up, that again also suck. And the production just sounds cheap and sounds like I have heard it thousands of times before, because I have. But their not all bad, there were some moments that I enjoyed at least a little bit, but for the most part, it's pretty bland.
label: negative

review: The production very repetitive and overall very uninteresting and uninspired. The lyrics are unbelievably generic and bland. The tracklist has some decent parts but even those are few and far between. Overall, very boring and bad album.
